Levels.ECOG: Electrode was a subdural grid or strip

notch.Description: Indicates whether a notch filter was applied. In this study, a notch filter was not applied to any channel

resection.Description: Indicates whether the electrode contact was located in the resected brain area

Levels.resected: Contact was within the resected area
Levels.preserved: Contact was within the preserved (non-resected) area

hemisphere.Description: Indicates the location of the electrode contact. “L” denotes the left hemisphere, and “R” denotes the right hemisphere.

lobe.Description: Indicates the location of the electrode contact.

Levels.frontal: The contact was located in the frontal lobe, excluding the precentral and paracentral gyri.
Levels.central: The contact was located in the precentral, postcentral, or paracentral gyrus.
Levels.parietal: The contact was located in the parietal lobe, excluding the postcentral and paracentral gyri.
Levels.occipital: The contact was located in the occipital lobe.
Levels.temporal: The contact was located in the temporal lobe.
Levels.insula: The contact was located in the insular cortex.

dataset.Description: Indicates whether the electrode contact was used in our study

Levels.trained: Electrode was trained to develop SOZ-prediction model (comprehensive HFA model) with XGBoost
Levels.unused: Electrode was not used in our study
Levels.other: Electrode was not used for developing SOZ-prediction model, but used for other analysis
